seoTitle: Wights Mountain vs Wigton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Wights Mountain and Wigton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Wights Mountain vs Wigton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wights Mountain and Wigton.
heading: Wights Mountain vs Wigton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wights Mountain, Queensland and Wigton, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wights Mountain has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Wigton (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wights Mountain or Wigton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wights Mountain has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Wigton scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wights Mountain scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Wigton scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wights Mountain or Wigton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wights Mountain has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
